title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
summary: An industry summit from June 2026 featuring leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and others discussing the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, the AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise AI budgets, cybersecurity implications, and the competitive compute race. Key themes include whether the recall constitutes an effective AI pause, concerns about China's advantage, the viability of announced data centers, and shifts from token optimization to outcome optimization.
writeup: This June 2026 summit gathers leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and others, including Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, and Aaron Levie, around the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models and what it means for the field. Panels weigh whether the recall amounts to an effective AI pause, raise concerns about China's competitive advantage, and question the viability of announced data centers amid the infrastructure buildout and compute race. Enterprise discussion covers AI budgets, cybersecurity implications, and a shift from token optimization toward outcome optimization. Recurring concepts include AI loops, frontier models, test-time compute, and multi-objective optimization.
